Bluetooth driver test functions for starting, stopping, and handling HCI passthrough and RF test modes.

# Purpose
This C source code file defines a set of functions for testing Bluetooth driver functionality. The functions include [`bt_driver_test_start`](<#bt_driver_test_start>), [`bt_driver_test_stop`](<#bt_driver_test_stop>), and [`bt_driver_test_enter_hci_passthrough`](<#bt_driver_test_enter_hci_passthrough>), which are likely used to control the start and stop of tests and to enter a specific test mode. The function [`bt_driver_test_handle_hci_passthrough_character`](<#bt_driver_test_handle_hci_passthrough_character>) processes characters in HCI passthrough mode, while [`bt_driver_test_set_spoof_address`](<#bt_driver_test_set_spoof_address>) sets a spoofed Bluetooth device address. The functions [`bt_driver_test_selftest`](<#bt_driver_test_selftest>) and [`bt_driver_test_mfi_chip_selftest`](<#bt_driver_test_mfi_chip_selftest>) perform self-tests, returning boolean values to indicate success or failure. The [`bt_driver_core_dump`](<#bt_driver_core_dump>) function is used to perform a core dump, which is a diagnostic operation. The file includes the header `bluetooth/bt_test.h`, suggesting that it is part of a larger Bluetooth testing framework.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `bluetooth/bt_test.h`
